skill.name: hyperframes-registry
skill.description: Install and wire registry blocks and components into HyperFrames compositions. Use when running hyperframes add, installing a block or component, wiring an installed item into index.html, or working with hyperframes.json. Covers the add command, install locations, block sub-composition wiring, component snippet merging, and registry discovery.
skill.summary: Install and wire HyperFrames registry blocks and components into video compositions
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 174 static findings are false positives. The skill uses markdown code blocks containing CLI command examples (hyperframes add, lint, preview). Hardcoded URLs point to legitimate HyperFrames GitHub registry and official CDNs. No command injection, data exfiltration, or malicious patterns detected. This is a legitimate open-source AI skill for video rendering composition authoring.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Block CLI Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: All flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are legitimate CLI command examples in fenced code blocks (bash, html, js). The skill demonstrates hyperframes add, lint, and preview commands. No actual shell execution or Ruby code exists.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All backtick patterns appear within markdown fenced code blocks (```bash, ```html, ```js) demonstrating legitimate CLI usage. No actual code execution exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Legitimate Hardcoded URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: Hardcoded URLs point to official HyperFrames resources: GitHub registry manifest, jsDelivr CDN for GSAP library, and HeyGen schema reference. No data exfiltration or malicious destinations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to known-safe official resources: heygen-com GitHub registry, jsdelivr CDN, and hyperframes.heygen.com schema.

content.user_title: Add HyperFrames Blocks and Components to Videos
content.value_statement: Adding animated blocks and visual components to video compositions requires knowing exact file paths, wiring attributes, and timeline integration. This skill provides guided workflows and worked examples for installing, wiring, and customizing HyperFrames registry items.
actual_capabilities: Install blocks and components using hyperframes add CLI command
Wire blocks into compositions via data-composition-src attributes
Merge component snippets into existing HTML, CSS, and JS
Discover available registry items via registry.json manifest
Customize installed blocks by editing data arrays and CSS rules
limitations: Requires hyperframes CLI to be installed (bun install -g @hyperframes/cli)
Cannot install examples, only blocks and components
Block and component customization requires manual HTML/CSS editing
Does not handle audio mixing or video encoding

use_cases.title: Add an Animated Chart to a Presentation Video
use_cases.description: Add a data-chart block to show animated bar and line charts in a business presentation video. Position the chart in a specific screen area using CSS.

use_cases.title: Add Visual Effects to Title Text
use_cases.description: Install the shimmer-sweep component to add a light sweep effect to headline text in a brand video.

use_cases.title: Build Multi-Scene Video Transitions
use_cases.description: Install the grid-pixelate-wipe component to create animated grid transitions between video scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Install a block
prompt_templates.prompt: Install the {block_name} block into my HyperFrames project using hyperframes add.

prompt_templates.title: Add a component
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the {component_name} component to my composition to create a {effect_type} effect.

prompt_templates.title: Wire a block into index.html
prompt_templates.prompt: Wire the data-chart block into my index.html so it appears at {time}s for {duration}s on track {index}.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to position a block in the timeline

prompt_templates.title: Browse available registry items
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all available blocks and components in the HyperFrames registry. I want to add {filter_type} effects to my video.
prompt_templates.scenario: When discovering what blocks or components are available

output_examples.input: Add the grain-overlay component to my video
output_examples.output: To add grain-overlay: Run `hyperframes add grain-overlay`. Open `compositions/components/grain-overlay.html`, copy the HTML div into your stage, paste the CSS keyframes, and add the class to your main composition's style block. The grain animates automatically via CSS @keyframes.

output_examples.input: Wire the flowchart block starting at 5s
output_examples.output: To wire the flowchart block: Add a div with `data-composition-src="compositions/flowchart.html"`, `data-composition-id="flowchart"`, `data-start="5"`, `data-duration="12"`, `data-track-index="1"`, and your canvas dimensions (e.g., 1920x1080).

best_practices: Run hyperframes lint after wiring to catch structural issues before previewing
Match block dimensions (data-width, data-height) to your composition canvas for proper scaling
Read the comment header in installed component files for customizable values like colors and timing
anti_patterns: Do not manually copy block HTML files without using hyperframes add - path remapping from hyperframes.json will be skipped
Avoid modifying installed block files directly if you plan to update from registry - customize via CSS overrides instead
Do not skip the data-composition-id attribute when wiring blocks - the runtime uses this to locate the composition

faq.question: What is the difference between a block and a component?
faq.answer: Blocks are standalone compositions with their own dimensions, duration, and GSAP timeline. Components are effect snippets pasted into your composition's HTML, CSS, and JS.

faq.question: How do I customize a block after installing it?
faq.answer: Edit the installed file directly. Data arrays are at the top of the script block. Colors are in CSS rules scoped under the block's data-composition-id attribute.

faq.question: Can I install items to a custom directory?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit hyperframes.json paths.blocks or paths.components to change install locations. Run hyperframes add after updating the config.

faq.question: Why is my block not appearing in the preview?
faq.answer: Check that data-composition-id matches the block's internal ID, data-start is within your composition duration, and data-composition-src path is correct.

faq.question: How do I discover what blocks and components are available?
faq.answer: Run `curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/heygen-com/hyperframes/main/registry/registry.json` to read the full registry manifest.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for examples, not just blocks and components?
faq.answer: No. For examples, use `hyperframes init <dir> --example <name>` instead. The hyperframes add command only works for blocks and components.
